critical.mandate: This is a PM-owned triage workflow — classify the change before cascading
Only cascade through AFFECTED artifacts — patches skip most steps
Cascade follows corrected lifecycle order: PRD → UX → Architecture → Test Plan → Threat Model → Traceability
Each cascade step spawns the owning agent's workflow as a subagent — do not perform edits directly
In yolo mode: auto-proceed through all steps EXCEPT CRITICAL Val findings which require user confirmation
On cascade failure: halt at the failed step, record partial state in assessment doc and checkpoint, present retry/skip/abort options (FR-89a)
Track cascade state via a lightweight manifest — do not hold subagent context in memory. Stay within the 40K token framework budget (FR-89b)

step.title: Intake
step.ask: Describe the fix, enhancement, or feature you want to add.
What is driving this change? (user feedback / stakeholder request / technical discovery / market shift / regulatory)
Urgency level? (critical — blocks current sprint / high — next sprint / normal — backlog)
step.action: Assign a feature ID: AF-{date}-{seq} (e.g., AF-2026-03-26-1). Check {planning_artifacts}/ for existing add-feature-*.md files to determine next sequence number.
Record: feature_id, description, driver, urgency

step.title: Classify
step.action:
Analyze the user's description against the classification matrix:
**Patch** — bug fix, typo, config correction. Characteristics: - Affects 1-2 files in a single module - No new requirements (FR/NFR) needed - No architecture changes - No UX changes - Existing tests cover the fix (or need minor additions)
**Enhancement** — improvement to existing feature. Characteristics: - Modifies existing requirements or adds minor new ones - May require architecture adjustments (MINOR) - May affect UX flows - Needs test updates for changed behavior
**Feature** — wholly new capability. Characteristics: - Adds new requirements (FR/NFR) - Requires architecture review (SIGNIFICANT) - Adds new UX flows or screens - Needs new test cases and possibly threat model updates
Determine classification: patch | enhancement | feature

Build the affected artifacts list based on classification: - **Patch**: PRD (optional — if wording fix), Traceability (if test changes) - **Enhancement**: PRD, UX (if UI changes), Architecture (if technical changes), Test Plan, Traceability - **Feature**: PRD, UX, Architecture, Test Plan, Threat Model, Traceability (all artifacts)
Present classification and affected artifacts list to user for confirmation.

step.title: Impact Scan
step.action: For each affected artifact, scan current content to identify specific sections impacted:
— PRD: identify affected requirement IDs (FR-*, NFR-*), feature sections, user personas
— UX Design: identify affected screens, interaction patterns, navigation flows
— Architecture: identify affected components, APIs, data models, ADRs
— Test Plan: identify affected test suites, risk areas, coverage gaps
— Threat Model: identify new attack surfaces or changed trust boundaries
— Traceability: identify requirement-to-test-to-story linkages that will change
Build impact summary: for each affected artifact, list specific sections and what will change.

step.title: Val Review
step.action: Present the proposed changes (classification, affected artifacts, impact summary) to Val for validation.
Invoke val-validate-artifact as a subagent, passing the impact summary as the artifact to validate.
If Val returns CRITICAL findings: present findings to user. In yolo mode, HALT and require user confirmation before proceeding. In normal mode, present [c]ontinue / [r]evise / [x]abort options.
If Val returns WARNING findings: present findings. In yolo mode, auto-proceed with findings noted. In normal mode, present options.
If Val returns no findings or INFO only: auto-proceed.

step.title: User Approval
step.ask: Review the triage plan above. Do you APPROVE or REJECT? [approve / reject / revise]
step.check: Set status = REJECTED. Skip steps 6-8 — proceed directly to step 9 (Assessment Doc) to record the rejection.

step.title: CR Record
step.action: Skip if classification is 'patch' — patches do not need a formal change request record.
For enhancements and features: create a change request record.
Assign CR ID: CR-{date}-{seq}. Check {planning_artifacts}/ for existing CR files.
Record: cr_id, feature_id, classification, driver, urgency, affected_artifacts, impact_summary.

step.title: Cascade
step.action: Initialize cascade manifest: ```yaml cascade_manifest: feature_id: "{feature_id}" classification: "{classification}" steps_total: {count of affected artifacts} steps_completed: 0 steps_failed: 0 status: "in_progress" steps: [] ```

Execute cascade in corrected lifecycle order, skipping unaffected artifacts:
**7a. PRD Update** (if affected) Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/2-planning/edit-prd/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} — {description}. Impact: {prd_impact}. Feature ID: {feature_id}." On success: record prd_diff (new FR/NFR IDs), update cascade manifest. On failure: record failure in manifest, present retry/skip/abort to user (FR-89a). If skip: note in assessment. If abort: halt cascade, save checkpoint.
**7b. UX Update** (if affected — skip for patches) Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/3-solutioning/edit-ux/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} — {description}. PRD changes: {prd_diff}. Impact: {ux_impact}. Feature ID: {feature_id}." On success: record ux_diff, update manifest. On failure: retry/skip/abort.
**7c. Architecture Update** (if affected — skip for patches) Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/3-solutioning/edit-architecture/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} — {description}. PRD changes: {prd_diff}. UX changes: {ux_diff}. Impact: {arch_impact}. Feature ID: {feature_id}." On success: record arch_diff, update manifest. On failure: retry/skip/abort.
**7d. Test Plan Update via test-design** (if affected — skip for patches unless test changes needed) Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/testing/workflows/edit-test-plan/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} — {description}. PRD changes: {prd_diff}. Architecture changes: {arch_diff}. Feature ID: {feature_id}." Alternative: use /gaia-test-design for full test plan regeneration when the scope warrants it. On success: record test_diff, update manifest. On failure: retry/skip/abort.
**7e. Threat Model Update** (if affected — features only, skip for patches and enhancements) Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/testing/workflows/threat-model/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} — {description}. Architecture changes: {arch_diff}. Feature ID: {feature_id}." On success: record threat_diff, update manifest. On failure: retry/skip/abort.
**7f. Traceability Update** (if affected) Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/testing/workflows/traceability/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: Regenerate traceability to include changes from {feature_id}." On success: record trace_updated, update manifest. On failure: retry/skip/abort.
After all cascade steps complete (or are skipped/failed): finalize cascade manifest with status summary.
Save cascade checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/add-feature-{feature_id}.yaml for /gaia-resume recovery.

step.title: Story
step.action: If classification is 'feature' or 'enhancement': spawn subagent to create implementation stories.

Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/4-implementation/add-stories/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} — {description}. New requirements: {prd_diff}. Architecture changes: {arch_diff}. Feature ID: {feature_id}."
When subagent returns: capture new story keys and epic assignments.
If classification is 'patch': create a single fix story inline or recommend /gaia-create-story.

Priority flag integration: if the feature driver is high-urgency (P0 priority, business-critical, or regulatory driver), set priority_flag: "next-sprint" in each created story's frontmatter. This signals sprint planning to auto-include these stories in the next sprint cycle. For non-urgent features, priority_flag remains null (default).

step.title: Assessment Doc
step.template-output: Generate assessment document with YAML frontmatter: --- feature_id: "{feature_id}" date: "{date}" classification: "{patch|enhancement|feature}" status: "{APPROVED|REJECTED}" urgency: "{critical|high|normal}" driver: "{source}" cr_id: "{cr_id or null}" --- Then detailed sections: - Description: what was requested and why - Classification: how it was classified with reasoning - Impact Analysis: per-artifact impact summary - Val Review: findings summary - Cascade Results: for each artifact — updated/skipped/failed with details - Stories Created: list of new story keys (or N/A for patches) - Cascade Manifest: final manifest state (steps completed, failed, skipped) - Recommended Next Actions: what to do next based on outcome If REJECTED: include rejection reason and impact analysis for future reference.

step.title: Next Steps
step.action:
Present summary and recommended next actions based on classification and outcome:
**Patch:** - If story created: "Run /gaia-dev-story {story_key} to implement the fix." - If no story: "The patch is straightforward — apply directly or create a story with /gaia-create-story."
**Enhancement:** - "New stories created: {story_keys}. Run /gaia-sprint-plan or /gaia-correct-course to schedule." - If architecture updated: "Architecture changes recorded. Review with /gaia-agent-architect."
**Feature:** - "Full cascade complete. New stories: {story_keys}." - "Run /gaia-sprint-plan to schedule implementation." - "Consider running /gaia-implementation-readiness to verify consistency."
